memoryArtifacts.description: Two quite different derivatives for this name, are given. Thomas G. Gentry in his Family Names[1] says it is from the Anglo Saxon Scir mann, scir, meaning a shire, county, district; mann, a man; and i …

memoryArtifacts.headline:
Edmund Sherman
(about 1572 – May 1641)
memoryArtifacts.description:
He came from Dedham, Co. Essex to Massachusetts Bay in 1635 (based on his election as Watertown selectman on Nov. 25, 1635). 

From Watertown, he removed to Wethersfield by 1638, and, finally, New Hav …

memoryArtifacts.headline: Additional Sherman Research and References
memoryArtifacts.description: The genealogies of Edmund Sherman found online are unbelievably confused and there were several men of the same name, father & son, cousins, nephews, etc.  What seems to be the most accurate source …

nameMeanings.name: Sherman
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (London): occupational name denoting someone who used shears to trim the surface of finished cloth and remove excessive nap, from Middle English sherman, shirman, sharman ‘shearman’.
Americanized form of North German Schürmann (see Schuermann ) and of German or Jewish (Ashkenazic) Schermann .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): occupational name for a tailor, from Yiddish sher ‘scissors’ + man ‘man’.

variants.name: Schuermann
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Franz, Guenter, Hans, Heiner, Klaus, Kurt, Udo.

North German (Schürmann): habitational name for someone from a place called Schüre(n), or a topographic name for someone who lived by a barn, from Middle Low German schüre ‘barn’. Compare Schurmann , Sherman , and Shurman .

variants.name: Shearer
meanings: Scottish and English: occupational name for a reaper of standing crops, a sheep shearer, or someone who used shears to trim the surface of finished cloth and remove excessive nap, from Middle English sherer(e) ‘shearer’, an agent derivative of Middle English schere(n) ‘to shear’. Middle English schere denoted shears and scissors of all sizes. Compare Sherman .
Americanized form of German or Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Scherer   or   Scherrer  , cognates of 1 above. Compare   Sharrer  .

variants.name: Shearman
meanings: English: variant of   Sherman  . The surname is also established in Ireland (Kilkenny).

nameMeanings.name: Edmund
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + mund ‘protector’. It was borne by several early royal and saintly figures, including a 9th-century king of East Anglia killed by invading Danes, allegedly for his adherence to Christianity. In the 16th and 17th centuries, there was a good deal of interchange between Edmund and Edward.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."
On April 24, 1638, a company of five-hundred English Puritans led by the Reverend John Davenport and Theophilus Eaton, a wealthy London merchant, sailed into the harbor.

en.lifeSummary: When Edmund Sherman was born about 1572, in Dedham, Essex, England, his father, Edmund Sherman, was 25 and his mother, Anne Pellett, was 25. He married Grace Makin about 1598. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 5 daughters. He died before May 1641, in New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America.

ko.lifeSummary: Edmund Sherman(이)가 대략 1572년 영국 에세스 데드함에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Edmund Sherman(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Anne Pellett(은)는 25세였다. 그는 대략 1598년 Grace Makin(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1641년 5월 이전 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 뉴 하븐에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Edmund Sherman родился около 1572 г. в местности дедхам, эссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Edmund Sherman было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Anne Pellett, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Grace Makin около 1598 г. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Он умер перед май 1641 г. в местности ню хавен, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика.
